The rupee's future direction depends on easing energy prices and global stability.

topStories.description: While none of us plans to wage a war, the philosophy behind Sun Tzu's quote lies a single, uncomfortable truth: Deception is not a bug in the system; it is the system.

dailyNews.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) separately claimed responsibility for striking the aircraft.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court quashes copyright case against filmmaker Sujoy Ghosh over his 'Kahaani 2' film
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court bench allowed Sujoy Ghosh's plea, challenging a Jharkhand High Court's order, which had refused to quash the proceedings pending against him.

dailyNews.title: Rs 1 lakh crore wiped off! Bajaj Finance shares tumble 18% in March so far amid raging Iran-US war
dailyNews.description: Bajaj Finance shares have plunged over 20% in a month, eroding more than Rs 1 lakh crore in market value amid escalating Iran-US tensions. Rising oil prices, inflation concerns and macroeconomic risks flagged by Moody’s have weighed on sentiment, dragging financial stocks lower and keeping investors cautious amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.

dailyNews.title: ‘Benjamin Netanyahu, will you end Iran war if Donald Trump asks you to?’ Israeli PM responds
dailyNews.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu dismissed death rumours at his first press conference since the Iran war began. He defended Israel's strategy against Iran and confirmed a commitment to avoid striking oil facilities.

dailyNews.title: FIFA’s Gianni Infantino seemingly changes course on peace efforts
dailyNews.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino said his organisation “can certainly not solve geopolitical conflicts.”

dailyNews.title: Donald Trump, Japan's Takaichi announce $40 billion US nuclear reactor project
dailyNews.description: President Trump and Japanese PM Takaichi announced a $40 billion nuclear power project in the southern US, featuring small modular reactors by GE Vernova and Hitachi.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court quashes FIR against YouTuber Elvish Yadav in snake venom case
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court quashed the FIR against YouTuber Elvish Yadav, citing legal insufficiencies in the allegations of snake venom use and drug involvement.

dailyNews.title: Man accused of shooting father in face with crossbow captured after snowy manhunt
dailyNews.description: A West Virginia man was arrested after allegedly shooting his father in the face with a crossbow and leading police on a snowy multi-county manhunt.

dailyNews.title: Media under fire: Journalists keep questioning Iran war as Hegseth calls them ‘unpatriotic’ and ‘anti-Trump’
dailyNews.description: War Secretary Pete Hegseth slams media coverage of Iran conflict as "dishonest" while President Donald Trump reportedly rebukes Israeli allies over gas field bombing.

dailyNews.title: Feds charge 3 in $2.5b scheme to smuggle us AI tech to China using dummy servers
dailyNews.description: Three men linked to Supermicro are accused of smuggling $2.5 billion in AI technology to China via fake documents and shell companies, prosecutors allege.

dailyNews.title: Missing University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey found dead in Barcelona
dailyNews.description: The body of University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey, 20, was recovered at Port Olimpic in Barcelona after a days-long search, his family confirmed.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine peace talks on ‘situational pause’ as Middle East conflict intensifies: Kremlin
dailyNews.description: The Kremlin says Ukraine peace talks are on a 'situational pause' as Zelenskyy signals a Ukrainian team could resume negotiations as soon as Saturday.

topStories.title: Media under fire: Journalists keep questioning Iran war as Hegseth calls them ‘unpatriotic’ and ‘anti-Trump’

topStories.title: Trump’s Complaint About Israeli Strike on Gas Field Exposes Divergent Strategies
topStories.description: President Trump said he told Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel he disapproved of the attack, which sent energy markets reeling. But Israeli officials said the Americans were informed beforehand.

topStories.title: Trump Says He Won’t Send Troops to Iran but Leaves Wiggle Room
topStories.description: The president was cagey about his plans for Iran. He confirmed the Pentagon was seeking $200 billion to support a protracted war effort while also claiming it would be over soon.

topStories.title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Prime Minister
topStories.description: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.

topStories.title: Using Charm and Restraint, Japan’s Leader Mostly Avoids Trump’s Wrath
topStories.description: During her first visit to the White House, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi drew praise from President Trump. But the war in the Middle East will test their relationship.

topStories.title: How Japan Reacted to Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
topStories.description: Some people criticized President Trump’s decision to invoke a painful chapter of history. Others worried it might harm U.S.-Japan relations.

topStories.title: A Gift From Trump to the Supreme Court
topStories.description: In a caustic critique of the court issued on social media late Sunday night, the president inadvertently buttressed its independence.

dailyNews.title: Judge Rules That R.F.K. Jr. Overstepped on Transgender Care
dailyNews.description: The ruling provides temporary relief for 21 states seeking to stop the Trump administration from ending federal funding to hospitals that provide gender-transition care.

dailyNews.title: Can Military Excellence Save Us From Trump’s Incompetence?
dailyNews.description: It should never have come to this.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Putting His Stamp on the World
dailyNews.description: Be careful what you wish for.

dailyNews.title: Late Night Pans Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
dailyNews.description: “Let me tell you: There is no doubt in my mind that everything he knows about Pearl Harbor begins and ends with a movie starring Ben Affleck,” Jimmy Kimmel said.

dailyNews.title: Federal commission made up of Trump appointees approves design of his gold coin
dailyNews.description: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the president and were appointed by him earlier this year, was without objection.

dailyNews.title: 3/19: CBS Evening News
dailyNews.description: Iran war escalates; Trump downplays war's impact on energy prices.

dailyNews.title: Rep. Carlos Giménez says "the uprising is starting" in Cuba
dailyNews.description: As President Trump talks of "taking Cuba," Republican Rep. Carlos Giménez of Florida is pushing for regime change. Giménez joins "The Takeout" to assess the state of the island's government.

dailyNews.title: Father of service member killed in Iran war said he never told Pete Hegseth to 'finish' the job
dailyNews.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and President Donald Trump have said the families of service members who died in the Iran war told them to finish the job.

dailyNews.title: Former FBI Director Comey subpoenaed in Trump-appointed prosecutor’s conspiracy probe
dailyNews.description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been subpoenaed in connection with an investigation being run by a Trump-appointed U.S. attorney. NBC News’ Ryan Reilly explains the probe dubbed the “grand conspiracy” investigation.

dailyNews.title: Some DHS contractors told White House officials they were asked to pay Corey Lewandowski
dailyNews.description: Some companies complained to the Trump administration that Lewandowski has stood to personally profit from the DHS contracting process. Lewandowski denies the allegations.

dailyNews.title: Trump invokes Pearl Harbor in front of Japanese prime minister to defend Iran attack secrecy
dailyNews.description: During the meeting, Trump praised Japan for "stepping up" to assist in efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz, "unlike NATO."

dailyNews.title: Trump invokes emergency powers with $23 billion in Gulf arms sales as Iran war wages on: WSJ report
dailyNews.description: The U.S. approved roughly $23 billion in weapons sales to three Gulf nations, moving to bolster their defenses as the Middle East war continued to escalate.
